summary: Critical inaccuracy in lens-index-storage.md key requirements (attributes DaemonClient API/socket details that don't exist in that spec); missing lens-cli-subcommands.md which is the actual source for DaemonClient::symbols(), IndexStatus.is_ready; code-analysis-service-v2.md relevance overstated at medium.

checklist_results.item: All affected crates/areas from pre-clarifications are covered by at least one spec
checklist_results.note: DaemonClient API (symbols(), index_status(), for_workspace()) is not covered — lens-index-storage.md was misattributed these details. The actual source spec (interfaces/lens/lens-cli-subcommands.md) is missing from the reference context.

checklist_results.item: Relevance scores are reasonable (high = directly implements, medium = related, low = background)
checklist_results.note: code-analysis-service-v2.md rated medium but is only tangentially related background — it covers the fillback LLM-enrichment service, not @spec annotation scanning. Should be low.

checklist_results.item: Key requirements listed per spec are accurate (match actual requirement IDs)
checklist_results.note: lens-index-storage.md key requirements are fabricated. The spec's actual content (R1-R4) is entirely about storage path resolution. No daemon socket, no DaemonClient, no IndexStatus, no symbols() in that spec.

checklist_results.item: spec_plan: every entry has main_spec_ref set (not null)
spec_plan: sections are reasonable for the requirements
checklist_results.note: overview, schema, logic, dependency, interaction, cli, test-plan, changes — appropriate for Phase 2 extension

checklist_results.item: spec_plan: modify entries have valid source paths
spec_plan: main_spec_ref paths include a subfolder (not root-level under crate)
spec_plan: each spec file covers exactly one logical unit (not multiple unrelated concerns)
spec_plan: no spec file would require duplicate section types (split into separate files if needed)
spec_plan: spec paths mirror source structure (interfaces/, logic/, generate/)

issues.description: lens-index-storage.md key requirements are inaccurate. The reference context claims this spec defines: 'socket at {root}/cclab/.index/daemon.sock', 'DaemonClient::for_workspace(root) constructs client from storage::resolve_socket_path()', 'Phase 2 calls DaemonClient::symbols(file) over the Unix socket', and 'IndexStatus.is_ready check needed before coverage analysis runs'. The actual spec (R1-R4) covers ONLY storage path resolution: {project_root}/cclab/.index/ directory, path canonicalization, SHA256 hashing, and module sub-path separation. None of DaemonClient, socket path, IndexStatus, or symbols() appear in lens-index-storage.md.
issues.recommendation: Correct the key requirements for lens-index-storage.md to reflect its actual content: R1 ({project_root}/cclab/.index/ storage root), R2 (path canonicalization), R3 (SHA256 hashing), R4 (module index separation).

issues.description: Missing spec entry: interfaces/lens/lens-cli-subcommands.md is not in the reference context, yet it is the actual spec defining DaemonClient::for_workspace(root), client.symbols(file), client.index_status() with is_ready flag, and the ensure_daemon_ready() pattern (R1-R2 of that spec). Phase 2's daemon interaction requires querying DaemonClient::symbols(file) — this spec is directly relevant.
issues.recommendation: Add interfaces/lens/lens-cli-subcommands.md to the reference table with relevance 'medium'. Key requirements: R1 (ensure_daemon_ready: DaemonClient::for_workspace(), index_status().is_ready, 30s timeout), R2 table entry (client.symbols(file) method signature).

issues.description: code-analysis-service-v2.md relevance is overstated at 'medium'. This spec covers the sdd_analyze_code_for_spec fillback tool — AST parsing + LLM enrichment for spec generation from source code. Phase 2's @spec annotation extraction is simple comment-line parsing, not this full pipeline. The claim 'same infrastructure as daemon' is not established by the spec itself.
issues.recommendation: Downgrade to 'low' relevance. Narrow key requirements to: R1 (multi-language tree-sitter support — background context that language parsers exist), R6 (fast-path AST-only — pattern reference only).
